title: Static lichen_iface pointer without thread-safety creates race with disable
description:
Files: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:71,286,325

The lichen_iface global is set in lichen_l2_iface_init() (line 325) and read in lora_rx_callback() (line 286) without synchronization.

If lichen_l2_enable(false) is called while the RX thread is mid-callback, the sequence could be:
1. RX callback reads lichen_iface (non-NULL)
2. L2 disable clears lichen_iface (not currently done, but logical cleanup)
3. RX callback calls lichen_l2_input with stale interface

Currently disable does not clear lichen_iface, but:
- A maintainer adding cleanup will create a race
- The pattern will be copied to similar state variables

Risk: Future maintainer adds 'lichen_iface = NULL' in disable path, causing null dereference in concurrent RX callback.

Fix: Document invariant (iface is never cleared once set) or protect with mutex.
